sourceMovie.title: I Saw the Devil
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit I Saw the Devil for the same reason it broke through in 2010: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a horror rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Kim Jee-woon keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The thriller and horror blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Visitor Q
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If I Saw the Devil clicked for you, Visitor Q (2001) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after I Saw the Devil?
faq.answer: Try Visitor Q, Body Cam, The Silence of the Lambs—each shares DNA with I Saw the Devil while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is I Saw the Devil?
faq.answer: I Saw the Devil (2010) is best described as Thriller, Horror. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is I Saw the Devil worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this thriller lane.
